title: arthraxon lanceolatus var. meeboldii
intro: Arthraxon lanceolatus var. meeboldii, also known as Indian oat grass, is a type of grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems. Indian oat grass is typically prepared in herbal teas, decoctions, or infusions, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine preparations.
